title: 10 Herbal Creams For Bleeding Gums
intro_desc: Herbal creams for bleeding gums can be a natural and effective solution to relieve pain and discomfort. These creams often contain herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight off infection. Herbal teas, on the other hand, can also play a role in relieving bleeding gums, but they work in a slightly different way. When you drink herbal tea, the active compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and carried to the affected area, where they can help to soothe and calm the gums.  For example, Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, is a popular herb used in herbal creams and teas to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the gums and reduce bleeding, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection from taking hold. Echinacea purpurea, also known as coneflower, is another herb commonly used to boost the immune system and fight off infection. Its active compounds help to stimulate the immune system, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums. Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is a herb that has been used for centuries to promote oral health and reduce inflammation. Its antiseptic properties help to kill bacteria and prevent infection, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and promote healing.  Using herbal creams or drinking herbal teas can have several benefits for your life. For one, they can help to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums, making it easier to eat and speak. They can also help to prevent infection and promote healing, reducing the risk of complications and promoting overall oral health. Furthermore, herbal remedies are often gentle and non-invasive, making them a great alternative to harsh chemicals and antibiotics. By incorporating herbal creams and teas into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to maintaining good oral health and preventing bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the gums. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and protecting bleeding gums. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and prevent tissue damage. The saponins in the cream help to break down and remove bacteria that can cause bleeding gums, while the carotenoids promote tissue repair and healing. Regular use of Calendula officinalis creams can help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and a reduced risk of gum disease.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat until it's warm and melted.
Add the dried Calendula officinalis flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the flowers.
Combine the strained mixture with 1/4 cup of beeswax and stir until the beeswax is fully melted and mixed in. Pour into a jar and let cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and flavonoids. These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in bleeding gums. Caffeic acid also has antimicrobial properties, which fight bacteria that can cause infections in the gums. Alkylamides help to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. By reducing inflammation and fighting infections, Echinacea purpurea creams may help to soothe and heal bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the solids and let the liquid cool slightly.
Add the vitamin E oil and essential oil (if using) to the cooled liquid. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains tannins, rosmarinic acid, and ursolic acid as its key bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight infection in the gums. Tannins, in particular, help contract blood vessels and reduce bleeding by constricting the blood vessels. Rosmarinic acid, on the other hand, has antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. The combination of these properties makes Salvia officinalis creams a potential remedy for bleeding gums, helping to soothe and heal the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine dried Salvia officinalis leaves and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 30 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and shea butter over low heat. Add the strained liquid and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Salvia officinalis cream is ready to use for bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains the bioactive constituents eucalyptol and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling and fight infection in bleeding gums. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptol and camphor help to reduce pain and inflammation, while also promoting healthy gum tissue repair. Eucalyptol also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. Camphor, on the other hand, helps to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected area, which can aid in the healing process. These properties make eucalyptus globulus creams a useful treatment for bleeding gums, as it helps to reduce pain, inflammation, and the risk of infection.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in the eucalyptus essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or container and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, your eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to your bleeding gums 2-3 times a day for relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in bleeding gums. The gel inside the aloe vera plant also contains vitamins A, C, and E, which are antioxidants that help fight off infection and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe barbadensis creams can also help reduce inflammation in the gums, allowing the gums to heal faster. The combination of these properties makes aloe barbadensis creams a good option for soothing and healing bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the aloe vera gel to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5-7 minutes. Then, stir in the distilled water and tea tree oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in bleeding gums. Flavonoids, in particular, have antioxidant properties that help combat free radicals and promote healthy blood vessels, which can contribute to gum bleeding. Bilobalide and ginkgolides also have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated gum tissue. By applying Ginkgo biloba creams to bleeding gums, these active constituents can work together to promote healing and reduce bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then add Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil. Mix well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely to form a solid cream. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including cineole, limonene, and terpineol. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat the underlying causes of bleeding gums. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can contribute to gum disease. Terpineol has antioxidant properties that help to protect gum tissue from damage. By targeting the root causes of gum bleeding, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to promote healthy gum tissue and reduce bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in the Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a silicone candy mold or a small tin container to shape the cream.
Let the mixture cool and harden completely before removing from the mold and applying to bleeding gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-annone that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera help soothe bleeding gums by reducing swelling and pain. The antimicrobial properties of aloin and aloe-emodin help fight off bacteria and fungi that can cause gum disease. Aloe vera also contains enzymes like bradykinase, which breaks down a protein called bradykinin that causes blood vessels to swell and lead to bleeding. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, aloe vera creams can help heal and protect bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
Add the coconut oil and mix until well combined. Then add the aloe vera gel and mix again.
Pour the mixture into a small bowl or container and let it cool until it solidifies.
Once solidified, your aloe vera cream is ready to use. Store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcuminoids, which are active compounds found in turmeric. Curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin are the main curcuminoids in Curcuma longa creams. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums. Curcuminoids also have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that can cause bleeding gums. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of curcuminoids in Curcuma longa creams can help soothe and protect the gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gum tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a saucepan.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in the saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture, stirring well.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify, then use it to massage into bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis creams contains berberine, hydrastine, and hydrastidin as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria in the gums. Berberine has a drying effect on tissues, which can help stop bleeding gums by reducing excess moisture and promoting faster healing. The antimicrobial properties of hydrastine and hydrastidin also help prevent infections that can worsen bleeding gums. The combination of these properties in Hydrastis canadensis creams makes it a potential treatment option for people with bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of Hydrastis canadensis root powder, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine distilled water and Hydrastis canadensis root powder in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring until the powder dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin E oil. Stir until the mixture is well combined.
Pour the mixture into a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir occasionally until the beeswax melts and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Pour the cream into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent bleeding gums from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the gums. They may also help reduce bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum problems. This can help keep gums healthy and strong.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm bleeding gums. However, consuming them daily might not be the best idea. Some herbs, like sage and chamomile, can be safe in moderation, but excessive consumption can cause stomach upset or interact with medications. It's essential to be mindful of the herbs you choose and their potential effects on your body.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bleeding gums, but it takes time. You might start to see improvements within a few weeks to a few months. Some people notice a difference in their gums within 2-4 weeks, while others may need to wait a bit longer. It's a gradual process.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for bleeding gums in the morning is a good idea. It helps prevent bleeding throughout the day. The antiseptic properties in the tea reduce inflammation and promote healing. This way, you can start your day with a healthy mouth and confidence.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for bleeding gums. Some herbs, like ginkgo biloba and garlic, thin the blood and may increase bleeding. If you're taking medications for bleeding gums, it's best to talk to your pharmacist about potential interactions with herbal teas. This can help you avoid any complications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have bleeding gums. Some herbal teas may interact with your body's hormones, which could affect your baby's development. If you want to try herbal teas, choose ones that are known to be safe during pregnancy, like peppermint or ginger, but always check the ingredients and ask the person who made the tea if it's okay.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a good option for soothing bleeding gums while breastfeeding. However, it's best to choose teas that are known to be safe during breastfeeding. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are often used for their calming properties and may help with gum pain. Just be sure to check the ingredients and avoid anything that could interact with breast milk.
